_schema.purpose: Per-NPC voice picks. All entries use Kokoro-direct synthesis — the F5-TTS voice cloning pipeline was fully retired on 2026-04-19 after confirming that Kokoro's non-English speakers produce intelligible English-with-accent (not phonetic gibberish) when fed English text. This eliminates cloning fragments and makes runtime live-TTS deterministically byte-identical to build-time prerender.

fidelity_levels.strong: Kokoro has a speaker that authentically matches the NPC (native accent or ideal timbre)
fidelity_levels.workable: Kokoro has a close-enough speaker — accent flavor or timbre is present but not canonical
fidelity_levels.placeholder: No direct match — using closest English Kokoro speaker; flagged for external sourcing

gaps_open.Russian: Keril + Mikhail Kolyatov — need Russian-accented ENGLISH (not Russian-language). Candidates: Mozilla Common Voice `accent='Russian'` samples, L2-ARCTIC is missing Russian but has adjacent Slavic options, or ElevenLabs paid voices tagged Russian.
gaps_open.German: Sergio Weisartz — need German-accented ENGLISH. Same sources.
gaps_open._note: Piper voices tagged ru_RU / de_DE / sv_SE were wrongly assumed to speak English with accent; in reality they're single-language trained and produce unintelligible output when fed English text. Reverted these to English-native placeholders on 2026-04-18.
gaps_open.Scottish: Elias McIntire — need Scottish-accented MALE ENGLISH. Previously used Piper en_GB-alba-medium but verified 2026-04-19 that Alba is female despite the Scottish-Gaelic name meaning 'Scotland'. Candidates: Common Voice accent='Scottish' samples, ElevenLabs Scottish male voices.

_schema.lessons_learned: Automated scoring (F0 rise, loudness, duration) cannot detect phonetic unintelligibility — a Piper voice happily produces correct F0 contours while saying nonsense words. Always audibly verify new reference voices before locking them into the mapping.
